Effective And Honest Scales

Goal

This notebook shows how scale choices affect interpretation.

It adapts the clinical-course example on effective and honest scales.

Analytic Questions

We will ask:

  • How can axis limits exaggerate or reduce apparent differences?
  • When should a scale start at zero?
  • How do we show a focused comparison without misleading the reader?

Load Data

flu_path <- "data/real/flu_vaccination_age_time_clean.csv"

flu <- read_csv(flu_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

Prepare Data

us_may <- flu |>
  filter(
    geography == "United States",
    month == 5,
    age_group %in% c("18-49 Years", "50-64 Years", ">=65 Years"),
    !is.na(estimate)
  )

A Focused Axis

p1 <- ggplot(us_may, aes(x = season_start_year, y = estimate, color = age_group)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(25, 80))

p2 <- p1 +
  labs(
    title = "Focused Axis For Comparing Seasonal Changes",
    subtitle = "United States, month 5",
    x = "Season start year",
    y = "Vaccination estimate (%)",
    color = "Age group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

p2

The focused axis makes year-to-year changes easier to see.

A Full Percent Scale

p1 <- ggplot(us_may, aes(x = season_start_year, y = estimate, color = age_group)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 100))

p2 <- p1 +
  labs(
    title = "Full Percent Scale Shows The Size Of The Estimates",
    subtitle = "United States, month 5",
    x = "Season start year",
    y = "Vaccination estimate (%)",
    color = "Age group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

p2

The full scale makes the absolute level clearer, but compresses small changes.

Bars Usually Need Zero

latest <- us_may |>
  filter(season_start_year == max(season_start_year)) |>
  mutate(age_group = reorder(age_group, estimate))

p1 <- ggplot(latest, aes(x = age_group, y = estimate)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip(ylim = c(0, 100))

p2 <- p1 +
  labs(
    title = "Bars Encode Length From A Baseline",
    subtitle = "Latest available season, United States, month 5",
    x = "Age group",
    y = "Vaccination estimate (%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

p2

Takeaways

  • Focused axes can help compare change, but the limits should be clear.
  • Percent scales often need context for the full 0-100 range.
  • Bars encode length from a baseline, so zero usually matters.
  • Scale choices should match the comparison the figure asks the reader to make.
